summary: Machine policy for hiding personalized website recommendations from Start. The local Microsoft StartMenu.admx file documents the same Explorer policy key and HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites value name used by the app.

setting.name: Start Personalized Site Recommendations
setting.casual_explanation: Windows 11 Start can surface personalized website suggestions. This device policy hides those recommendations for everyone on the machine.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want less personalized content in Start or want Start to behave more like a static launcher.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding personalized sites reduces suggestion-driven content, but users lose a Windows-provided shortcut surface for personalized web recommendations.
setting.professional_notes: This is a both-scope Start policy documented by the Start Policy CSP and StartMenu.admx. The app's machine variant currently writes the HKLM policy value through a command-backed registry tweak.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The device does not force personalized site recommendations off.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites = 1 under HKLM, using a command-backed registry tweak, which matches the documented hide behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default

states.rationale: The device policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Start Policy CSP documents a functional default of 0, but the policy baseline here remains the missing value for restore-default semantics.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want the ordinary Windows 11 Start experience
avoid_for: Devices where personalized Start suggestions are not wanted
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep deciding whether to surface personalized sites in Start.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a less personalized Start menu.
tradeoffs: Personalized site suggestions can remain available.
No device-wide policy override is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Less personalized Start
intended_for: Users who want fewer personalized suggestions
avoid_for: Users who like Start surfacing personalized websites

states.rationale: Hide personalized site recommendations in Start.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Start to feel more static and less personalized.

evidence.title: Microsoft Start Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for HideRecommendedSection and HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites, including allowed values and scope.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft StartMenu.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps HideRecommendedSection and HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites to the Explorer policy key with both user and machine scope.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites = 1 under HKLM for this tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft StartMenu.admx file explicitly defines HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer with the same value name the app writes, and the official Start Policy CSP documents the 0/1 semantics for the same policy.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same Explorer policy key and HideRecommendedPersonalizedSites value used by the app. The official Start Policy CSP provides the 0/1 shown-versus-hidden semantics for the same policy.
